title: A Historic First: Marine Corps Reserve Integrates Multidomain Assets During Exercise
description: The Marine Corps Reserve is actively deploying multidomain collection assets during an integration course for the first time in its history, marking a technological milestone that ensures the service remains an agile force prepared to dominate in complex, data-driven conflicts.

body.text: For the first time in its history, the Marine Corps Reserve is actively deploying multidomain collection assets during an integration course, marking a significant technological milestone for Integrated Training Exercise 3-26 at Marine Corps Air Ground Combat Center in Twentynine Palms, California.

The Information and Intelligence Integration Course is a rigorous, three-day evolution that provides a progressive training opportunity for Marine Air-Ground Task Force 23 intelligence and information entities to rehearse complex tactics, techniques and procedures associated with advanced equipment operation, data exchange and battlespace awareness.

Marine Corps Lance Cpl. Josia Baltirra, assigned to the 1st Battalion, 24th Marine Regiment, 4th Marine Division, feeds ammo to an M240B machine gun during Integrated Training Exercise 3-26 at Marine Corps Air Ground Combat Center in Twentynine Palms, Calif., June 10, 2026.
